9.2     Comparison of the Two Designs



9.2.1     Design Value Comparisons



        The components of the design value for Shearon Harris and

Shearon Harris II power plant have been calculated in chapter 5

and 6.     The quantities for the following are compared:

  - Measure of Modularity

  - Module Alignment Value

  - Pipe Penalty Routing Value

  - Concrete Volume Value

  - Modular Function Value.



        The value for each of the DV components above are shown in
Table 9-1.     With these values a relative percentage between the

two designs     is also calculated   (SH   II vs. SH).    Design Value
components are weighted according to their relative economic

contribution.     This is with the coefficients in the design value

so that an equitable representation of value and cost is

            - Embedments

            - Structural Supports.

        All of these cost estimates include the costs of labor in

performing each specific task.              The results of this        analysis are
reported in the next section.



9.3.2      Results of Cost Estimates



       The first component of the DV to be estimated here is the

MOM.       The MOM is the cost of connecting piping systems at the

plant after the module has been installed.                      It is assumed that
this    connection       is   done   with    a    standard      bent   or   straight
section.       As    a   rough    estimate       half   of    the   connectors     are
straight and        half the      connectors are        bent.       For non-nuclear
safety      (NNS) class piping the bent section is composed of one

elbow and two straight sections.                 They are shop fabricated in

the site     factory and delivered to the power plant as modules are

installed.       For     safety    grade    piping      the   piece    is   a   single
section bent to a radius according to the pipe diameter (R=5xD)                     .
The final cost of the MOM for the two plants is the following:

(Table 9-2)



   -   Shearon Harris MOM            = 1.125 million dollars

   - Shearon Harris II MOM = 0.621 million dollars.



       It should be noted that the cost for the pipe length

 the Reactor Auxiliary Building and its associated systems.                                  The

 RAB systems and their associated matrix identification number

 are shown in Table 6-2.



 6.2.4     Step 3 - Matrix Analysis of Systems



         With    the    list    of     major    plant       systems     (piping/equipment

 systems) completed the interfaces between them can be evaluated

and with the Cost Penalty Matrix clustering analysis.                                 For each

system      tables       of     interfaces            and     their     pipe    size/safety

classification          are     generated.             With    this     information         each
interface        is    given      an    economic        penalty        according       to    the
procedure described              in Chapter 2.              This     information      is    then
placed in a symmetric matrix form to relate each system to every

other one.        For the RAB case studied here the input matrix is

shown in Fig.          6-1.

       After      running        this     input        matrix       through     the     matrix

reordering       program        an     output    matrix        of    grouped    systems       is
produced as shown in Fig. 6-2.                   The clustered systems from this

matrix are then given priority for adjacent placement                                   in the
plant while the those systems which do not cluster or have low

values     are    given       lower     priority       in     the plant       layout.        The
determination of the clustered boundaries                             in this matrix are

left   to the individual designer.                We selected cluster boundaries

that     encompassed          numerically       large       adjacent     matrix       elements
since they       represent        systems with          high        interaction    and      cost
